title: oxalis crocea
intro: Oxalis crocea, also known as the Golden Wood-sorrel, is a type of flowering plant in the Oxalidaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Oxalis crocea are believed to include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms associated with urinary tract infections, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the Oxalis crocea plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
